items.briefing:
Cybersecurity experts are warning that AI is making attacks faster and harder to defend against, with both Acronis and Check Point highlighting how criminals are using advanced AI tools to launch more sophisticated threats. This is a timely reminder that no organisation is too small to be targeted, including training providers handling student data and financial information.

Separately, a security flaw has been discovered in Ubuntu Linux that could allow attackers to gain full control of affected systems. If your organisation uses Ubuntu on any computers or servers, ensure your IT team applies the latest updates immediately.

**Practical tip:** Enable automatic software updates on all devices to reduce the risk of known security flaws being exploited.

featured.title: Ubuntu snap-confine flaw can give attackers root access
featured.quote: Critical Ubuntu vulnerability allows local attackers to gain root access on default systems.
